activities_projects.title: Chapter Summary Writing
activities_projects.objective: Develop the ability to summarize the story
steps: Read the chapter carefully
Identify the key events from the author’s childhood
Write a summary in 8–10 sentences
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and completeness of summary (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Character Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the personality of the author as a child
steps: List the traits and qualities of the author in childhood
Give examples from the text illustrating these traits
Write 5–6 sentences describing the child’s personality
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and clarity of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Event Sequencing Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the sequence of events in the author’s childhood
steps: List important incidents from the childhood described
Arrange them in chronological order
Write one sentence explaining each incident

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Builder
activities_projects.objective: Learn new words from the chapter
steps: Find 8 new words from the chapter
Write their meanings
Use each word in a meaningful sentence

activities_projects.title: Personal Reflection Writing
activities_projects.objective: Relate to the author’s childhood experiences
steps: Think about a memory from your own childhood
Write 6–8 sentences describing it
Compare it with the author’s experiences in the chapter
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops reflective and expressive writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity, relevance, and personal insight (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Dialogue Writing
steps: Imagine a conversation between the child author and a friend or parent
Write at least 6–8 lines of dialogue
Include discussion about daily childhood activities
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Improves creative writing and dialogue skills

activities_projects.title: Theme Identification
activities_projects.objective: Identify the main theme of the chapter
steps: Think about the importance of childhood, learning, and innocence
Write the main theme in 3–4 sentences
Give examples from the chapter supporting the theme
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops understanding of the story’s moral and deeper meaning
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and relevance of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Play Activity
steps: Select a scene from the chapter
Act the roles of the child author and another character
Practice the dialogue with classmates

activities_projects.title: Creative Illustration
steps: Draw a scene described in the chapter
Include expressions and environment
Write a short description of the scene
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and clarity of visualization (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Opinion Writing
activities_projects.objective: Express personal views about the story
steps: Write your opinion about the author’s childhood experiences
Explain whether they are relatable or inspiring
Support your ideas with examples from the chapter
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops opinion writing and reasoning skills
